title: Kane to miss Bayern game against Gladbach with calf knock
description:
BERLIN: Harry Kane will miss Bayern Munich’s Bundesliga match against Borussia Moenchengladbach with a calf injury, coach Vincent Kompany said on Thursday.
	The England striker, who has been an ever-present for Bayern this season, should be fit in time for the Champions League last-16 first leg at Atalanta next Tuesday.
	“Harry Kane is out. He got a knock on his calf and hasn’t recovered yet,” Kompany said at a pre-match press conference ahead of

title: PSG prepare for Chelsea clash with Monaco rematch
description:
PARIS: Paris Saint-Germain warm up for their Champions League last-16 showdown with Chelsea by once again taking on Monaco on Friday, as coach Luis Enrique looked forward to “the best part of the season” starting.
	PSG have not managed to rediscover the exhilarating form that led them to Champions League glory for the first time in their history last season, but they come into March still on course to retain their French and European titles.

title: Swedish intelligence warns of increased Middle East war fallout
description:
STOCKHOLM: A top Swedish intelligence official on Thursday warned of increased threats against American and Israeli interests, as well as Iranian dissidents, in Sweden because of the war in the Middle East.
	The Scandinavian country’s security, which had already deteriorated after Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, has worsened since the US-Israeli strikes on Iran on Saturday unleashed a new war, according to Fredrik Hallstrom, head of operations for

title: Israel decided to kill Khamenei in November, defense minister says
description:
JERUSALEM: Israel took the decision to kill Iran’s Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in November and was planning to carry out the operation around six months later, Defense Minister Israel Katz said on Thursday.
	Khamenei was killed in the first hours of the US-Israeli air campaign that began on Saturday in the first assassination of a country’s top ruler by an airstrike.

title: Macron calls on Israel, Iran and Hezbollah to prevent Lebanon being embroiled in conflict
description:
LONDON: Emmanuel Macron on Thursday called on Israel and Iran not to embroil Lebanon in the conflict sweeping the Middle East.

	The French president made his plea as panic swept through Beirut after Israel ordered residents to evacuate the southern suburbs of the Lebanese capital and three villages in Lebanon’s eastern Bekaa region.

	Macron said he had drawn up a plan to end hostilities between Israel and the Iran-backed Lebanese militia Hezbol

title: Jordan’s king condemns Iranian attacks in region during talks with Syrian president
description:
LONDON: King Abdullah of Jordan and President Ahmad Al-Sharaa of Syria discussed on Thursday the dangerous developments in the region this week following the US and Israeli attacks against Iran that began on Saturday.

	During their telephone conversation, both leaders emphasized the need for dialogue to prevent further escalation of the regional crisis.

title: El-Sisi says Egypt in ‘state of near-emergency’ as war threatens economy
description:
CAIRO: Egyptian President Abdel Fattah El-Sisi said Thursday his country was in an economic “state of near-emergency” as a result of the Middle East war, warning of runaway inflation.
	The Arab world’s most populous nation has not been physically impacted by the US and Israeli war with Iran, which has seen strikes on Egypt’s wealthy Gulf allies and paralyzed trade through the vital Strait of Hormuz.

title: How Israel’s ‘buffer zone’ plan could redraw the map of southern Lebanon
description:
BEIRUT: Lebanon faces what officials described as “one of the most dangerous moments in recent memory” after Hezbollah launched attacks on Israeli forces from areas both south and north of the Litani River, triggering a renewed Israeli air and ground campaign.

	Announcing its ground incursion into southern Lebanon, Israel said it aims to establish what it called a permanent “security zone” along the border.

title: Europol warns Iran crisis raises threat of terror, extremism and cyberattacks
description:
MADRID: The Middle East conflict will have “immediate repercussions” for European Union security with an increased threat of terrorism, serious and organized crime as well as violent extremism and cyberattacks, European police body Europol told Spanish news agency EFE on Thursday.
	Europol spokesman Jan Op Gen Oorth said he expected to see more cyberattacks against European infrastructure and an increase in online fraud using increasingly sophist

title: Hezbollah drone hits Metula home amid wave of attacks, nobody hurt; ministers urge escalation
description:
IDF warns residents of 10 south Lebanon towns to leave ahead of strikes on terror group; Ben Gvir, Smotrich demand major attacks, as Aoun calls Israeli withdrawal 'non-negotiable'
The post Hezbollah drone hits Metula home amid wave of attacks, nobody hurt; ministers urge escalation appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Daily Briefing May 25: Iranians tell ToI they are losing hope as ceasefire turns stalemate
description:
Diplomatic reporter Nava Freiberg parses out the buzz and blowback in the US and Iran over the potential memorandum of understanding
The post Daily Briefing May 25: Iranians tell ToI they are losing hope as ceasefire turns stalemate appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Iran and US voice optimism but temper expectations for imminent breakthrough in talks
description:
Tehran: Understandings reached on many issues, nuclear issue will only be discussed after sanctions relief; Rubio: Deal could be 'today,' but no hurry, Israel 'always has right to protect itself'
The post Iran and US voice optimism but temper expectations for imminent breakthrough in talks appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: ‘10 Agams in Iran’: The secret story of Israeli and Jewish art in the regime’s hands
description:
The Tehran Museum of Contemporary Art owns pieces by the iconic master of kinetic art and several other Jewish artists. While works occasionally go on display, most are shrouded in mystery
The post ‘10 Agams in Iran’: The secret story of Israeli and Jewish art in the regime’s hands appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Left-wing activist, whom settlers shoved on camera, to be questioned under caution
description:
Peace Now says its CEO Lior Amichai now a suspect, while settlers are not; clash took place after tour of West Bank Bedouin hamlet whose residents fled following settler attacks
The post Left-wing activist, whom settlers shoved on camera, to be questioned under caution appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Experts warn of European vulnerability to drone threat
description: Europe has been rocked by incursions into its airspace by rogue Ukrainian loitering munitions and unattributed drones in recent weeks.

title: US withdraw more than 4,000 troops from Europe
description: The US Department of War has announced the withdrawal of a Brigade Combat Team, comprising at least 4,000 troops, from Europe.

title: France swelters as first-ever May heatwave alert issued
description: France experienced record high temperatures for May over the weekend, according to the country’s national weather service, with Paris recording this year's first temperature above 30C on Saturday. A spokesperson for Meteo France added that a yellow heatwave alert issued Sunday was the very first “to be issued in May since the system was created in 2004".

title: Ebola outbreak raises fears of wider spread in Central Africa
description: An Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda is raising fears of wider spread, with over 900 suspected cases and more than 200 deaths reported in the DRC. Health workers warn the toll is likely higher due to limited testing, while conflict, mistrust, and lack of treatment for the rare strain are hampering containment efforts.

title: Runners collapse as temperatures soar across France
description: An unusually early heatwave has pushed temperatures above 30°C across France, leading to multiple medical emergencies during running events. In suburbs of Paris and other cities, dozens of participants collapsed, with several hospitalised in serious condition and emergency services overwhelmed by heat-related incidents.

title: 'Self-destructive' Putin loses home support as Ukraine war rages on
description: PRESS REVIEW – Monday, 25 May 2026: First, papers discuss whether Russian President Vladimir Putin has lost support from his backers. Next, a police raid of opposition party headquarters in Turkey is getting considerable coverage. Also, the Enhanced Games do not lead to the many breakthroughs that were expected, with only one new world "record". Meanwhile, a 99-year-old breaks records in the sky. Finally, actors called Chris and talking animals a

title: Philippines: Deadly building collapse traps workers in Manila
description: A nine-storey building under construction collapsed in the Philippine capital Manila, killing several people and leaving around a dozen missing, most of them construction workers trapped under the rubble. Rescue operations remain difficult because of the unstable structure, while authorities revealed the project had previously been suspended over safety violations before work resumed last year.

title: 'Work in progress': Washington seeks diplomatic exit from Iran war
description: Despite signs of progress, uncertainty still surrounds a possible agreement between Washington and Tehran, with both sides signalling caution over the terms of any final deal. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said on Sunday that negotiations were still a “work in progress”, though his comments suggested growing confidence that a breakthrough could eventually be reached. FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert has more.

title: Oil prices ease on renewed hopes of US-Iran peace deal
description: Oil futures slid on Monday as traders assessed US President Donald Trump's announcement over the weekend that a deal with Iran had been "largely negotiated". The benchmark Brent crude still remains much higher than when the war started on February 28. Also in this edition: some commercial vessels are managing to cross the Strait of Hormuz. Plus, Ferrari is set to launch its first fully electric supercar model.

title: The mullet makes a comeback at European championship
description: The mullet hairstyle is enjoying a revival, with around 2,000 fans gathering in Belgium for the European Mullet Championship. Beyond the haircut itself, participants described the mullet as a symbol of freedom, individuality and resistance to conformity.

title: Millions gather in Mecca for Hajj despite security concerns
description: Despite security concerns linked to the conflict in the Middle East, pilgrims from around the world continue to arrive in Mecca for this year’s Hajj. Saudi officials say more than 1.5 million people have already reached the kingdom, with numbers expected to rise further as many say they are willing to take the risk to complete the pilgrimage.

title: Middle East: Deep mistrust clouds US-Iran negotiations
description: Rouzbeh Parsi, Adjunct Senior Lecturer at Lund University, is FRANCE 24’s guest to discuss the evolving situation in the Middle East. Iran and the United States are both seeking a possible diplomatic way out of the conflict, but uncertainty remains high amid conflicting signals from both sides. Parsi highlights the contradictions in the current negotiations and the difficulty in finding a credible agreement, particularly as mistrust continues to

title: Uncertainty persists over US-Iran draft agreement
description: Iran and the United States say progress has been made in talks over a 14-point memorandum of understanding, but both sides insist a final deal is not imminent. While US officials, including Secretary of State Marco Rubio, describe the process as a “work in progress,” Tehran has accused Washington of sending mixed messages and says key details are still unresolved.

title: The world urgently needs a US-Iran deal now
description: Without a deal between Washington and Tehran, the fallout from Strait of Hormuz closure could deepen global crises.

title: China’s Xi praises ‘unbreakable’ Pakistan ties as Sharif visits Beijing
description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif says China and Pakistan are iron brother countries.

title: Trump’s war loop: Escalate, retreat, repeat
description: Stuck in a quagmire of a war, Trump chafes at negative coverage while Iran digs its heels in.

title: Surveillance video shows moment of China coal mine blast
description: Surveillance video caught the moment a powerful explosion surged through the tunnels of a coal mine in China.

title: Israel kills three in attacks on Lebanon, issues more displacement orders
description: Israel carries out attacks on multiple villages and towns in southern Lebanon.

title: Abandoned flotilla boat washes ashore in Egypt with Gaza aid
description: A small boat believed to be part of the Global Sumud Flotilla that was carrying aid for Gaza has washed ashore in Egypt

title: Senegal's leadership row mounts as parliament speaker resigns
description: Some speculate that El Malick Ndiaye stepped down so that ousted-PM Ousmane Sonko can take his place.

title: At least 19 people taken to hospital after 'strong smell' reported at Tokyo mall
description: Police say a man sprayed a substance at an ATM in the luxury Ginza 6 shopping centre.

title: Anger grows after China's deadliest coal mining disaster in years
description: On China's tightly-controlled internet, people are calling for justice and questioning how this happened.

title: She was told to marry in a country which bans girls' education. So she got in a taxi and fled
description: Nearly five years on from Afghanistan's school ban, young women say they have waved goodbye to their dreams.

title: A deal with the U.S. would be preliminary and focused on ending war, an Iranian official says.
Deal with US not imminent, Iran says
description: The US secretary of state earlier said that an agreement could possibly come on Monday.

title: Iran War Live Updates: Peace Deal Could Take Days to Nail Down
description: Oil prices fell sharply Monday, as negotiations between the United States and Iran appeared to continue. But both sides have offered conflicting accounts of the emerging agreement.

title: Malaysia appoints new anti-graft chief
description:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia appointed on Saturday a former high court judge as the country’s new anti-graft czar to replace its outgoing chief who was dogged by allegations of abuse of power and illegal shareholding.
	The appointment of Abdul Halim Aman comes as Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim’s government faces increased scrutiny on promises made to tackle corruption in the Southeast Asian nation.

title: Chinese panda pair headed to US zoo
description:
BEIJING: Two giant pandas from China are headed to Atlanta on a new 10-year conservation deal.
	Pandas Ping Ping, a male, and Fu Shuang, a female, will live at Zoo Atlanta, China Wildlife Conservation Association said in a statement Friday.
	The organization signed the research agreement with the United States zoo last year, it said, noting the deal continues “the ‘panda bond’ shared by the people of both nations for more than 20 years.”

title: Despite Iran tensions, King Charles III will follow his mother’s lead in celebrating US-UK bonds
description:
LONDON: The challenge for King Charles III as he embarks on this week’s state visit to the US is, as always, to live up to his mother’s example.
	The late Queen Elizabeth II wowed Congress in 1991 with a speech that celebrated the shared democratic traditions of Britain and the United States, quoted Abraham Lincoln, Franklin D. Roosevelt and Ralph Waldo Emerson, and highlighted the deep bonds between the two nations.

title: Australia refuses repatriation help for citizens in Syria camp
description:
SYDNEY: Australia refused to help repatriate citizens related to suspected Daesh militants on Saturday, following a report that some Australian families are trying to return home from a camp in Syria.
	National broadcaster ABC said four Australian women and nine of their children and grandchildren had left the Roj camp in northeastern Syria on Friday, citing the camp director.
	Syrian forces were taking them to Damascus to help them fly back to A

title: Soldier killed in south Lebanon by Hezbollah drone, another seriously wounded
description:
Sgt. Nehoray Leizer, 19, from Eilat, was driving armored personnel carrier when it was struck in the Bint Jbeil area; he is 10th soldier killed amid truce, 23rd since March 2
The post Soldier killed in south Lebanon by Hezbollah drone, another seriously wounded appeared first on The Times of Israel.

description:
Rubio: Agreement still possible today, ‘Israel always has a right to protect itself’ * Australia’s spy chief: Antisemitism was left unchecked after Oct. 7, became normalized

title: Arab parties urge Ra’am to join efforts to revive Joint List; Ra’am tepidly agrees
description:
Hadash, Ta'al, Balad seek unity due to 'existential dangers' to Arab public and despite 'significant gaps' with Islamist party, which says it must retain its freedom of action

Testifying to inquiry into Bondi massacre, ASIO head says lack of response to hatred 'gave more permission for violence,' some celebrated the Hamas onslaught before Israel responded

description:

description:
Millions of Muslims from around the world flock to Saudi Arabia's holy city, though summer scorcher raises heatstroke risk as authorities look to cool down the massive crowds

title: Israel began the Iran war as a partner of the US — and is ending it on the sidelines
description:
Netanyahu said the war was meant to halt the Iranian threat and suggested it brought the US and Israel closer than ever. Now Trump is freezing the PM out of talks that may leave that threat intact
The post Israel began the Iran war as a partner of the US — and is ending it on the sidelines appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Anti-Semitism was 'left unchecked’ in Australia after Gaza war, spy chief says
description: Australia’s spy chief on Monday told an inquiry into the Bondi Beach mass shooting that anti-Semitism was left unchecked in the country after the start of the war in Gaza. Australian Security Intelligence Organisation head Mike Burgess added that before the attack that killed 15 people attending a Jewish Hanukkah celebration in December 2025, anti-Semitism had escalated in severity from “intimidating behaviour to direct targeting of people”.

title: Premier League: Tottenham beat Everton to stay in top flight
description: Tottenham beat Everton on the final day of the Premier League season and secured their place in the league. Chelsea will miss out on all European competitions.

title: Middle East war live: Iran says agreement with US ‘not imminent’ despite progress
description: Iran's foreign ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei warned on Monday that an agreement with the US to end the war was “not imminent”, despite Tehran and Washington making progress "on a large portion of the issues" in their exchanges. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio earlier said that a deal could materialise "today". Follow our liveblog for all the latest updates.

title: California declares emergency as fire crews race to contain toxic leak
description: Thousands have been evacuated as officials warn a tank containing about 7,000 gallons of methyl methacrylate could explode.

title: Iran war day 87: Trump says US not in rush to sign deal, dashing optimism
description: Iranian media are reporting that a deal may not be reached amid disagreements on some key issues.

title: Rubio says US will find ‘another way’ if Iran talks fail
description: US secretary of state says a pretty solid deal is on the table in terms of opening up the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Gaza flotilla activists return to Australia, describing abuse
description: Australian activists from the Gaza-bound aid flotilla have arrived back in Sydney, reuniting with loved ones.

title: Singapore’s economy beats expectations as gov’t warns of Iran war fallout
description: GDP grows 6 percent year-on-year in first quarter as AI boom outweighs rising energy costs.

title: Blast targeting train kills at least 20 in Pakistan
description: Officials say the train had been transporting military personnel home for Eid when it was attacked by armed separatists.

title: RAF jet carrying defence secretary has signal jammed near Russian border
description: Pilots on board had to use a different navigation system while the plane's GPS was disabled.

title: Kyiv, Ukraine, Hit in Russian Missile Attack
description: Buildings rattled in the Ukrainian capital for hours early Sunday. Russia launched an Oreshnik intermediate-range ballistic missile for only the third time in the war.

title: Oil prices slide on hopes of US-Iran peace deal
description: Trump said on Saturday that an agreement would include the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, without giving further details.

title: Large-scale Russian attack on Ukraine leaves four dead and dozens injured
description: A hypersonic missile, which reportedly travels over 10 times the speed of sound, was used, Russia has confirmed.

title: Rubio Rebukes Hezbollah Chief Over Call for Lebanese to ‘Take to the Streets’
description: The militant leader, in a speech, had criticized the Lebanese government for engaging in direct talks with Israel.

title: Rubio says 'solid' Iran deal may come on Monday
description: The deal under discussion would involve a 60-day ceasefire extension during which the Strait of Hormuz would be reopened, US media reports.

title: U.S.-Iran Peace Deal Nearer, But Could Take Days to Nail Down, U.S. Official Says
description: The official said the sides had agreed in principle to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and commit Iran to disposing of its highly enriched uranium, but stressed that a deal had not yet been signed.

title: Главные новости к утру 25 мая
description: США и Иран достигли принципиального соглашения об открытии Ормузского пролива, сообщает The New York Times со ссылкой на источник. ВСУ обстреляли Белгород ракетами.

title: Gaza mosques announce death of Hamas military leader after Israeli targeting claim
description:
CAIRO: Mosques in northern Gaza ​on Saturday announced that Hamas’ military wing commander had died, a day ‌after Israel’s ‌military ​said ‌that ⁠it ​had targeted ⁠the armed wing chief in airstrikes.
	Witnesses said that mosques in ⁠Gaza City ‌had ‌announced ​Izz Al-Din ‌al-Haddad’s “martyrdom.” There ‌was no immediate comment from Hamas on the fate ‌of the militant group’s military ⁠chief.
	Israel ⁠has not said if he was killed in the air strike

title: Putin to visit China May 19-20, days after Trump trip
description:
MOSCOW: Russian President Vladimir Putin will travel to China on May 19 for a two-day visit, hot on the heels of US President Donald Trump’s trip to Beijing, the Kremlin said on Saturday.
	During the trip, the Russian leader will discuss with his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping how to “further strengthen the comprehensive partnership and strategic cooperation” between Moscow and Beijing, according to a Kremlin statement.
	Putin and Xi will “exchan

title: Iraq exported 10 million barrels of oil through Strait of Hormuz in April
description:
BAGHDAD: Iraq exported 10 million barrels of oil via the Strait of Hormuz in April, down from about 93 million barrels monthly before ​the Iran war, the country’s new oil minister, Basim Mohammed, said at a press conference on Saturday.
	The closure of the Strait of Hormuz due to the Iran war has curtailed oil exports from Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Kuwait and Iraq, sending prices sharply higher.

title: Israel strikes south Lebanon day after ceasefire extension
description:
BEIRUT: Israel launched new airstrikes against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon on Saturday, a day after the two countries agreed to extend a truce following talks in Washington.
	“The IDF has begun striking Hezbollah infrastructure sites in several areas in southern Lebanon,” the Israeli military said.
	Lebanon’s state-run National News Agency (NNA) reported strikes on at least five villages in the south, preceded by an exodus of residents toward t

title: SFD continues providing financial aid to countries amid geopolitical tensions
description:
RIYADH: Sudan, Cameroon and Palau were among countries that have received financial support from the Saudi Fund for Development so far in 2026, as it continued its work despite growing geopolitical tensions.

	Established in 1974 and commenced operations in 1975, SFD has financed more than 800 development projects in over 100 countries, with a cumulative value exceeding $22 billion.

title: Palestinian ministry says Israeli forces kill man in West Bank camp
description:
RAMALLAH: Palestinian health officials said Israeli forces killed a man on Saturday on the outskirts of the Jenin refugee camp in the northern occupied West Bank.
	The health ministry in Ramallah identified the victim as 34-year-old Nour Al-Din Kamal Hassan Fayyad, saying he was “killed by occupation forces’ fire in the Jenin camp.”
	The Palestinian Red Crescent said its teams in Jenin received a man “with no signs of breathing or pulse from insi

title: Awaiting deal in Iran war, British navy prepares to clear mines in Strait of Hormuz
description:
Hundreds of British sailors in Gibraltar are getting ready to secure the vital oil pathway alongside France after US-Iran deal inked; clearing entire strait could take years
The post Awaiting deal in Iran war, British navy prepares to clear mines in Strait of Hormuz appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Exclusive: What's inside the Iran deal Trump is close to signing
description: The agreement the U.S. and Iran are close to signing involves a 60-day ceasefire extension during which the Strait of Hormuz would be reopened, Iran would be able to freely sell oil, and negotiations would be held on curbing Iran's nuclear program, according to a U.S. official. Why it matters: The deal would avoid an escalation of the war and decrease the pressure on the global oil supply. However, it's unclear whether it will lead to a lasting p

title: Israel orders remote-controlled SMASH Hopper from Smart Shooter
description: The Israeli Ministry of Defense has agreed a deal with Smart Shooter for SMASH Hopper lightweight remote-controlled weapon stations.

title: Middle East war live: Rubio says Iran deal still possible Monday
description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Monday that a deal to end the war with Iran could materialise "today", adding that Israel had the right to defend itself against attack. Follow our liveblog for all the latest updates.

title: Africa Day 2026: Has the continent achieved true liberation?
description: Africa Day 2026 spotlights power, debt and digital control as Africans redefine sovereignty.

description: Russia provided a rare look at damage caused by a Ukrainian strike on a college in occupied Luhansk

description: Countries are learning that the finer things in life sometimes have a serendipitous side benefit.

title: Trump reportedly asked Muslim, Arab leaders to normalize ties with Israel if Iran deal reached

Request was met with silence on call with Middle Eastern leaders, Axios reports; it comes as Israeli officials said alarmed that emerging deal 'does not serve Israel's interest'

title: Russia launches unannounced nuclear exercise, including Belarusian launch sites
description: The dual messaging − nuclear muscle-flexing at home, high-profile diplomacy abroad − fits a pattern the Kremlin has used repeatedly.

title: Rubio ratchets up pressure on NATO at key summit
description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is casting new doubt on NATO's relevance after key allies recoiled from backing Washington’s “Operation Epic Fury.”

title: Novak Djokovic keeps record 25th grand slam dream alive with French Open comeback win
description: Novak Djokovic rallied from a set down to keep alive his bid for a record 25th Grand Slam title at the French Open on Sunday, overcoming France's Giovanni Mpetshi Perricard in the opening round. Alexander Zverev advanced more comfortably as the clay-court major got underway in Paris.

title: California declares state of emergency after toxic leak threatens thousands of residents
description: California authorities said on Sunday they had discovered a crack in a damaged tank containing toxic chemicals, easing concerns over a potential explosion that prompted the evacuation of about 40,000 residents. The leak began on Friday in Orange County's Garden Grove area, sending fumes across a densely populated region near Disneyland.

title: LIVE: Trump says Iran deal not ‘fully negotiated yet’
description: US president says any potential agreement with Tehran will be good and proper as mediation efforts continue.

title: In Ukraine, a Divisive 20th-Century Hero Comes Home
description: Andriy Melnyk, criticized as a Nazi collaborator and lionized as an anti-Soviet resistance leader, was given state honors for his reburial near Kyiv.

title: NYT: США и Иран договорились об открытии Ормузского пролива
description: США и Иран достигли принципиального соглашения об открытии Ормузского пролива, сообщает The New York Times (NYT) со ссылкой на источник. Детали договоренностей еще предстоит проработать, и еще неизвестно, останется ли у Ирана какой-либо контроль над морским коридором, пишет NYT.

title: Trump fires homeland security chief Kristi Noem
description:
WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump on Thursday fired Kristi Noem as head of the Department of Homeland Security, the agency responsible for carrying out his sweeping immigration crackdown.
	According to multiple media reports, Trump was upset with Noem’s testimony at a Senate hearing this week where she said the president had approved a $220 million DHS advertising campaign in which she featured prominently.
	Trump, in a post on Truth Social,

title: Scoop: Democrats eyeing 2028 bids court Warren
description: Massachusetts Sen. Elizabeth Warren, one of most influential leaders in the progressive movement, is being publicly and privately courted by Democrats eyeing presidential bids in 2028 — a sign they're trying to build up their lefty credentials.Why it matters: Progressives are on the rise in the party, and Warren is a key ally to have. She lost her bid for the White House in 2020 but played a major role in shaping Joe Biden's presidency by install

title: High Court implores justice minister to appoint district court judges
description:

title: Dems weaponize Epstein in anti-GOP midterm ads
description: Several top Democratic candidates in the midterms are airing scathing ads linking their Republican foes to the Jeffrey Epstein scandal — betting that the Trump administration's reluctance to release the Epstein files still resonates with voters.Why it matters: Democrats are mostly focusing on high prices, health care and Trump's war against Iran, but some also are trying to tie Republicans to the late sex offender as part of a broader message acc

title: At least two killed as Russia uses hypersonic Oreshnik missile in mass attack on Kyiv area
description:
Russia says the large-scale attack, using drones and missiles, was retaliation for Ukrainian strikes on 'civilian facilities on Russian territory'
The post At least two killed as Russia uses hypersonic Oreshnik missile in mass attack on Kyiv area appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Trump said to assure Netanyahu he will thwart Iran nuke program amid jitters over emerging deal
description:
Israel said worried plan falls short of key security needs with Iran's nuclear activities only up for discussion later, and a halt in Lebanon fighting that leaves Hezbollah armed and dangerous

title: Iran’s Pezeshkian says Tehran ready to assure world it’s not seeking nuclear weapons
description:

title: Russian Fighters Made More ‘Dangerous Intercepts’ of a Royal Air Force RC-135 over the Black Sea

title: Sweden chooses Naval Group’s FDI frigate for Luleå-class programme
description:
Sweden’s Ministry of Defence has chosen French Naval Group to supply the Royal Swedish Navy with four defence and intervention (FDI) frigates under the Luleå-class programme.
The post Sweden chooses Naval Group’s FDI frigate for Luleå-class programme  appeared first on Naval Technology.

title: Germany touts pan-German space command amid European push to supplant US tech
description: Austria’s Defense Minister Claudia Tanner reaffirmed that Austria plans to put three operationally designated military satellites into orbit next year.

title: Italy rethinks EU defense-financing aid as arms spending falls out of fashion
description: Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni wants to refocus the bloc's help on lowering the cost of living for Italians, as energy costs have soared amid the Iran war.

title: The president who threatened to end a civilization is supposed to guarantee Ukraine’s survival
description: Trump's Iran war has diverted diplomats and drained interceptor stockpiles — just as his envoys were supposed to deliver security guarantees to Kyiv.

title: 13 US troops killed, more than 380 wounded in Operation Epic Fury
description: In the 40 days since the start of the Iran War, 13 U.S. service members have been killed and 381 have been wounded, according to U.S. Central Command.

title: Poland joins Pentagon’s counter-drone marketplace amid unexpected US deployment cancellation
description: The Army is continuing to expand military cooperation with Poland through the Pentagon’s counter-drone initiative, the service announced on Wednesday.

title: Russia, Ukraine to enter temporary ceasefire with prisoner exchange, Trump says
description: President Donald Trump said Friday that Russia and Ukraine would temporarily halt fighting for three days beginning Saturday.

title: US, Iran launch new attacks as they wrestle for control of Gulf waters
description: It was the first apparent attempt to use military force since last month’s ceasefire announcement.

title: The week in pictures: Ebola outbreak in Africa, Putin in China and Cannes Film Festival mermaids
description: The Ebola outbreak spreads in Democratic Republic of Congo, Russian President Vladimir Putin and his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping meet in Beijing, Japanese actress Arisa Sasaki dresses as a mermaid for the Cannes Film Festival and Gaza flotilla activists are humiliated by Israel’s far-right National Security Minister Ben-Gvir. Here is a look back at some of the week’s most striking images.
